# Initialize Govern

Prepare the current project to use Wonder Govern capabilities.

## Procedure

scope

- Resolve the current project root.
- Resolve the current execution surface.
- Reject unsupported execution surfaces before writing files.

prepare

- Ensure `.wonder/` exists.
- Ensure `.wonder/config/` exists.
- Ensure `.wonder/standards/` exists.
- Ensure `.wonder/runs/` exists.
- Create `.wonder/config/govern.json` only when it is absent.
- Create `.wonder/standards/coding.md` only when it is absent.
- Create `.wonder/standards/architecture.md` only when it is absent.
- Create `.wonder/standards/security.md` only when it is absent.
- Create `.wonder/standards/docs.md` only when it is absent.
- Starter standards must contain minimal placeholder content and must not overwrite existing user content.

register

- Read `.wonder/state.json` when present.
- If `.wonder/state.json` is invalid JSON, stop without overwriting it and report that registry repair is required.
- Merge only the `wonder-govern` registry section.
- Register `init`, `define-standards`, and `check-policy` capability surfaces for every supported execution surface.
- Mark only the current execution surface as initialized.
- Preserve other plugin registry sections.

report

- Report created paths.
- Report existing paths reused.
- Report registered capability ids.
- Report starter standards files created.
- Report the current execution surface initialized state.

Do not create `.wonder/reports/govern-latest.json`.
Do not define full project standards.
Do not check policy compliance.
Do not modify other plugin config files.
Do not initialize other plugins.
